title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph's viral essay explores why some women are reducing their boyfriends' online presence.
The essay suggests women want to distance their identities from traditional heterosexual relationships.
Chanté discusses her essay's impact on relationship perceptions on the Love Letters podcast.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O'Neill is running the Boston Marathon for The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp, aiding kids with serious conditions.
The camp provides a medically safe environment for children to enjoy activities, free of charge, fostering joy and independence.
O'Neill's fiancée's sister, a former camper, now in remission, serves as a counselor at the camp.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Eagles have decided not to trade wide receiver A.J. Brown at this time despite interest from the Patriots.
Trade discussions may resume closer to June 1, when salary cap relief could make a deal more favorable for the Eagles.
The Patriots offered a first- and third-round draft pick for Brown, which was declined by the Eagles, seeking more value.

title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum to play against the Washington Wizards after resting Thursday for Achilles injury management.
Tatum has been on a minutes restriction since his return from a ruptured Achilles, playing 27 minutes in each game.
Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable, while Nikola Vucevic is out for the Celtics' matchup with Wizards.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'War Machine' featuring Alan Ritchson now streaming on Netflix, follows a soldier excelling at Army Ranger training.
'Zootopia 2' with Jason Bateman and Ginnifer Goodwin is available on Disney+, continuing their police adventures.
Nicole Kidman stars in 'Scarpetta' on Prime Video, a murder mystery linked to her character's first case.

title: 5 things to know about Patriots offensive lineman Alijah Vera-Tucker
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Alijah Vera-Tucker joins the Patriots after being a first-round pick for the Jets, bringing versatility to the offensive line.
Vera-Tucker, 26, has faced injury setbacks, missing 42 of 85 potential games since being drafted in 2021.
Morgan Moses influenced Vera-Tucker's decision to join the Patriots, citing team culture and a winning atmosphere.

title: Alex Cora identified 2 players he views as leaders of the 2026 Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox manager Alex Cora names Trevor Story and Garrett Crochet as clubhouse leaders for the 2026 season.
Trevor Story played 157 games last year, leading the team in home runs, RBI, runs, and hits.
Garrett Crochet led MLB in strikeouts in 2025 and is compared to elite pitchers like Randy Johnson by Cora.

title: 4 things to watch during Boston Legacy FC’s NWSL debut against Gotham FC
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC makes its NWSL debut against Gotham FC at Gillette Stadium on March 14, 2026.
Head coach Filipa Patão aims for a possession-style game despite potential defensive vulnerabilities against Gotham FC.
The match will be played on newly-installed grass at Gillette Stadium, favoring Boston's planned methodical play style.

title: Should Massachusetts stop changing its clocks?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers are debating a bill to end the twice-a-year clock change and adopt a permanent time system.
Senate President Karen Spilka and State Senator John Keenan support the shift to Atlantic Standard Time to improve public health.
Research links twice-yearly time changes to increased risk of heart attacks and accidents, but daylight hour adjustments could impact New Englanders.

title: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Boston Pride For The People announces 2026 parade and festival date as June 6, starting at Copley Square.
The celebration includes a parade, concert at Boston Common from noon to 6 p.m., and a block party from 2 to 8 p.m.
Mayor Michelle Wu supports the event, emphasizing joy and inclusivity; volunteers are sought through the group's website.
